item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A explicitly disagrees with the statement but consistently argues from a community-responsibility and social-harmony frame rather than a strongly individual-rights frame. Agent B consistently rejects society-over-individual priority and defends individual autonomy. Both agents respond directly to each other's claims; no visible reversal or clear concession occurs. Agent B turn 4 contains a non-English/non-Latin fragment in 'The印尼 argument', but the judgment scores only argumentative content.
